fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Missouri Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity missouri, how to transfer audiologist license to missouri, audiologist compact missouri, missouri audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Advisory Commission for Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists
board.mailing_address: 3605 Missouri Boulevard, P.O. Box 4, Jefferson City, MO 65102

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Missouri.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Register as a new user on the MO Professional Registration Portal. Once registered, log in and complete the online application for Audiologist licensure, providing all required personal and professional information.

steps.title: Submit Transcripts and Exam Scores
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts verifying your master's or doctoral degree in audiology to be sent directly from your academic institution to the Missouri Board of Healing Arts. Also, ensure your passing scores for the Praxis Exam in Audiology are sent directly from ETS to the Board.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Clinical Experience Verification (if applicable)
steps.description: If your degree program did not include one year of supervised clinical experience, submit Form A (Verification of Supervised Clinical Experience) completed by your supervisor to the Missouri Board of Healing Arts.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board to complete the required criminal background check via fingerprinting. Specific instructions and approved vendors are typically provided after starting the application.

steps.title: Verify Out-of-State Licenses (if applicable)
steps.description: If you hold or have held audiologist licenses in other states, arrange for verification of those licenses using Form B to be sent directly from the respective licensing boards to the Missouri Board of Healing Arts.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $75 through the MO Professional Registration Portal as part of the online application process.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Missouri audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Missouri have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Missouri participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Missouri without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Missouri?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Missouri
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Missouri, you must submit your application and supporting documents through the MO Professional Registration Portal, managed by the Missouri Board of Healing Arts.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the MO Professional Registration Portal URL, click 'New User? Register Now', and follow prompts to create an account using your email and personal information. Once registered, log in and select the application for Audiologist Licensure.
